item.title: backlog edit --groomed-at accepts invalid date values without validation
item.intent: backlog edit --groomed-at must reject non-date values — silently storing garbage in groomedAt corrupts item metadata and is inconsistent with backlog history --since which validates the same YYYY-MM-DD format.
item.description: In cmd_backlog_edit(), the --groomed-at case at lines 3934-3945 accepts any non-empty value that doesn't start with '--' as a valid date. There is no format check. Running 'backlog edit FEAT-001 --groomed-at notadate' silently stores 'notadate' in groomedAt. In contrast, cmd_backlog_history() validates its --since date argument against ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$ before using it (lines 4022-4024). The groomedAt field is displayed in backlog show and used for tracking grooming activity, so invalid values undermine tooling built on this field. Fix: add the same regex validation used in cmd_backlog_history() --since.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_edit() and locate the --groomed-at case (around line 3934)
After reading gdate from $2, add a format check: if \! [[ "$gdate" =~ ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$ ]]; then log_error "Invalid date format: $gdate (expected YYYY-MM-DD)"; return 1; fi
This check should only apply when the user provided a value (shift 2 path), not the default-to-today path (shift 1 path)
Verify: 'backlog edit <ID> --groomed-at baddate' errors; 'backlog edit <ID> --groomed-at 2026-01-01' works; 'backlog edit <ID> --groomed-at' sets today's date
acceptanceCriteria: backlog edit <ID> --groomed-at notadate exits non-zero and prints an error about invalid date format
backlog edit <ID> --groomed-at 2026-03-20 still sets groomedAt correctly
backlog edit <ID> --groomed-at (no value) still uses today's date as the default
Error message includes expected format: YYYY-MM-DD
